rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [4+2] annulation-rearrangement-aromatization of styrenes to multi-substituted naphthalenes mediated by TBPA, forming polycyclic aromatics from simple styrene building blocks
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of TBPA (tris(4-bromophenyl)amine) to a stable radical-cation TBPA•+, which then oxidizes electron-rich styrene to its radical cation via SET

key_intermediates.name: radical cyclization adduct (70) - C-C bonded dimeric cation-radical
rearranged tertiary carbocation (75) after para-anisole migration
cathodic H2 evolution from HFIP/proton reduction

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes TBPA to TBPA•+, which performs SET on electron-rich styrene to give styrene radical cation 69; a second styrene attacks 69 (radical cyclization) to form 70, deprotonation/further oxidation yields dehydrodimer 68; TBPA•+ oxidizes 68 to radical 73 then cation 74, which undergoes 1,2-aryl (para-anisole) migration to tertiary cation 75, and oxidative aromatization delivers naphthalene 2. Protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: isolated dehydrodimer 68 (intermediate resubjection)
probe_reagents.observation: 68 detected by GC during reaction, isolated in 50% yield; resubjecting 68 to electrolysis gave product 2 in 62% yield, confirming it as an on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 68 and of TBPA/1 mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Two anodic peaks for 68 at 1.45 V and 1.90 V (sequential 2e- losses); CV of TBPA + 68 indicates TBPA mediates oxidation of 68, paralleling TBPA/1 behavior

notes: Para-anisole 1,2-migration is favored over methyl migration due to electron-rich aryl, generating the more stable tertiary carbocation 75 prior to aromatization. HFIP serves as proton source/solvent enabling cathodic H2 evolution. Mechanism is proposed without radical-trap experiments (no TEMPO/BHT reported on these pages) or DFT; key support comes from CV and isolation/resubjection of intermediate 68.
